title: Unhandled Promise Rejection
body:
## Definition

An **unhandled promise rejection** occurs when a [Promise](concept:promise) rejects and no `.catch()` or `await` in try/catch handles it.

## Why it exists

Prevents silent async failures in production.

## How it works

```js
Promise.reject("oops"); // unhandled in Node may crash process
```

Browsers: `window.addEventListener("unhandledrejection")`. Always end chains with `.catch()` or try/catch around `await`.

## Common confusion

Forgotten `await` still runs the async function but errors surface as unhandled rejections, not sync throws.

## In practice

Global handlers for logging (Sentry); never empty async event handlers without error handling.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

An **[unhandled promise rejection](concept:unhandled-promise-rejection)** occurs when a [Promise](concept:promise) rejects and no [.catch()](concept:promise) or [await](concept:async-await) in [try](concept:try-catch-finally)/catch handles it.

## Why it exists

Prevents silent [async](concept:async-await) failures in production.

## How it works

```js
Promise.reject("oops"); // unhandled in Node may crash process
```

Browsers: `window.addEventListener("unhandledrejection")`. Always end chains with [.catch()](concept:promise) or [try](concept:try-catch-finally)/catch around [await](concept:async-await).

## Common confusion

Forgotten [await](concept:async-await) still runs the [async](concept:async-await) function but errors surface as [unhandled rejections](concept:unhandled-promise-rejection), not sync throws.

## In practice

Global handlers for [logging](concept:observability) (Sentry); never empty [async](concept:async-await) [event handlers](concept:typescript-react-events) without error handling.
